pr_summary: This PR modifies python code and introduces 18 high-severity issue(s) requiring attention.
verdict_reason: Found critical severity issue: SQL Injection Vulnerability.

findings.line_content: +    query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}"
findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
findings.description: The query is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks because it directly inserts user input into the query string.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.

findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
findings.description: The query is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks because it directly inserts user input into the query string.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.

findings.title: Insecure Deserialization
findings.description: The use of pickle.loads() can lead to code injection attacks if the input is not trusted.
findings.suggestion: Use a secure deserialization method, such as json.loads(), and validate the input data.

findings.title: Swallowed Exception
findings.description: Exception is caught with except Exception: pass, hiding failures from callers and logs.
findings.suggestion: Log the exception and re-raise or return an error response instead of pass.

findings.title: N+1 Query
findings.description: The list_all_users function executes a separate query for each user ID, resulting in a large number of database queries.
findings.suggestion: Use a single query to retrieve all users, such as SELECT name, email FROM users

findings.title: Unnecessary Loop
findings.description: The list_all_users function uses a loop to retrieve each user individually, which can be inefficient for large datasets.
findings.suggestion: Use a single query to retrieve all users, such as SELECT name, email FROM users

findings.title: N+1 Query
findings.description: A database query runs inside a loop, causing one query per iteration at scale.
findings.suggestion: Fetch all rows in a single query instead of querying inside the loop.

findings.title: Hardcoded Credentials
findings.description: Hardcoded database password in the code
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secure secrets management system to store sensitive credentials

findings.title: Exposed Secret
findings.description: Hardcoded API secret in the code
findings.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secure secrets management system to store sensitive secrets

findings.line_content: +    query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}"
findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.description: User input is directly inserted into the SQL query, making it vulnerable to SQL injection attacks
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection

findings.title: Hardcoded Credentials
findings.description: Database password is hardcoded in source code and exposed to anyone with repo access.
findings.suggestion: Load DB_PASSWORD from environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Secret
findings.description: API secret is hardcoded in source code and can be extracted from the repository.
findings.suggestion: Load API_SECRET from environment variables or a secrets manager.

findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.description: User input is directly inserted into the SQL query, making it vulnerable to SQL injection attacks
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection

findings.title: Unsafe Deserialization
findings.description: Using pickle to deserialize user input can lead to arbitrary code execution
findings.suggestion: Use a safe deserialization method, such as JSON or a secure serialization library

findings.title: SQL Injection
findings.description: User input is interpolated into a SQL string via f-string, enabling SQL injection.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT ... WHERE id = ?', (user_id,))

findings.title: Unsafe Deserialization
findings.description: pickle.loads() on untrusted input allows arbitrary code execution.
findings.suggestion: Use json.loads() or a safe token validation scheme instead of pickle.

findings.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability

findings.suggestion: Test that update_profile handles SQL injection attempts by using parameterized queries

positive_observations: The diff is scoped to a specific module with clear function boundaries.
Added code uses familiar patterns for the language and framework.
At least one database operation uses parameterized queries.
missing_tests: Test that get_user raises an exception when requester_id does not match user_id
Test that get_user handles SQL injection attempts by using parameterized queries
Test that update_profile handles SQL injection attempts by using parameterized queries
Test that load_session handles malicious input by using a secure deserialization method
Test that list_all_users handles a large number of users by using a more efficient querying method
Test that delete_user handles specific exceptions and provides informative error messages
